en.extract:
The velvet flycatcher (Myiagra eichhorni) is a species of bird in the family Monarchidae.
It is endemic to New Hanover, New Ireland & New Britain.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.


Taxonomy
The velvet flycatcher was formally described in 1924 by the German ornithologist Ernst Hartert based on a specimen that had been collected on the island of New Hanover in the Bismarck Archipelago by the Australian farmer Albert Frederic Eichhorn (1882-1931). Hartert considered the specimen to represent a subspecies of the Mussau flycatcher which he placed in the genus Monarcha and coined the trinomial name Monarcha hebetior eichhorni. The velvet flycatcher is now one of around 20 species placed in the genus Myiagra that was introduced in 1827 by Nicholas Vigors and Thomas Horsfield.
Two subspecies are recognised:

M. e. eichhorni (Hartert, EJO, 1924) – New Hanover (=New Hanover Island), New Ireland, New Britain and Watom (north of northeast New Britain) (east Bismarck Archipelago)
M. e. cervinicolor (Salomonsen, 1964) – Dyaul Island (south of northwest New Ireland, northeast Bismarck Archipelago)
The subspecies M. e. cervinicolor has sometimes been considered as a separate species, the Dyaul flycatcher.


References

The '''velvet flycatcher''' ('''''Myiagra eichhorni''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Monarchidae]].
It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[New Hanover Island|New Hanover]], [[New Ireland (island)|New Ireland]] & [[New Britain]].

Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.

==Taxonomy==
The velvet flycatcher was [[species description|formally described]] in 1924 by the German ornithologist [[Ernst Hartert]] based on a specimen that had been collected on the island of [[New Hanover Island|New Hanover]] in the [[Bismarck Archipelago]] by the Australian farmer Albert Frederic Eichhorn (1882-1931). Hartert considered the specimen to represent a [[subspecies]] of the [[Mussau flycatcher]] which he placed in the genus ''[[Monarcha]]'' and coined the [[trinomial name]] ''Monarcha hebetior eichhorni''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Hartert | first=Ernst | author-link=Ernst Hartert | year=1924 | title=The birds of St. Mattias Island collected by A.F. Eichhorn | journal=Novitates Zoologicae | volume=31 | pages=261–275 [271] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/33919501 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Cottrell | editor2-first=G. William | year=1986 | title=Check-list of Birds of the World | volume=11 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=525 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14484226 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=eichhorni | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=eichhorni | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=22 November 2024 }}</ref> The velvet flycatcher is now one of around 20 species placed in the genus ''[[Myiagra]]'' that was introduced in 1827 by [[Nicholas Aylward Vigors|Nicholas Vigors]] and [[Thomas Horsfield]].<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=August 2024 | title=Monarchs | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/monarchs/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=22 November 2024 }}</ref>

Two subspecies are recognised:
* ''M. e. eichhorni'' ([[Ernst Hartert|Hartert, EJO]], 1924) – New Hanover (=[[New Hanover Island]]), [[New Ireland (island)|New Ireland]], [[New Britain]] and Watom (north of northeast [[New Britain]]) (east [[Bismarck Archipelago]])
* ''M. e. cervinicolor'' ([[Finn Salomonsen|Salomonsen]], 1964) – [[Dyaul Island]] (south of northwest [[New Ireland (island)|New Ireland]], northeast [[Bismarck Archipelago]])

The subspecies ''M. e. cervinicolor'' has sometimes been considered as a separate species, the Dyaul flycatcher.<ref name=ioc/>
